name: XActions
description: ⚡ The Complete X/Twitter Automation Toolkit — Scrapers, MCP server for AI agents (Claude/GPT), CLI, browser scripts. No API fees. Open source. Unfollow people who don't follow back. Monitor real-time analytics. Auto follow, like, comment, scrape, without API.

commit.message:
feat: add X profile scraper with replies — browser console tool

- Two-phase scraping: feed scroll + navigate into each post for reply comments
- Floating draggable control panel (pause/resume/stop/export/download)
- Start/end post range filtering by URL or ID
- Content filters: whitelist, blacklist, date range, engagement thresholds
- Export formats: JSON, CSV (posts + replies), Markdown, text, HTML
- CSV splits posts and replies with ParentPostID linking

commit.message: feat: add profile + replies scraper for sentiment analysis

feat: add browser console cashtag search scraper

- Paste-into-DevTools script for scraping X cashtag search results
- Floating dark-themed control panel (Start/Pause/Resume/Stop/Export)
- Date range filtering (start/end date pickers)
- Auto-scrolls search results with deduplication
- Scrapes replies for each post via SPA navigation
- Built-in sentiment analysis (bullish/bearish/neutral)
- Export as JSON or CSV with aggregate sentiment + top tickers
- Draggable/minimizable panel, real-time progress stats

Add xeepy Python toolkit (complete codebase) to python/

All xeepy files preserved — scrapers, actions, AI, analytics, CLI,
monitoring, storage, docs, configs, site, tests.
Python companion to the JavaScript toolkit.

commit.message:
Add A2A API and Protocol Documentation, Client Models, and Authentication Module

- Created `a2a-api-reference.md` for comprehensive A2A API documentation, detailing endpoints for agent card, health checks, skills, tasks, orchestration, agent discovery, authentication, and error codes.
- Developed `a2a-protocol.md` outlining the implementation of Google's A2A protocol for inter-agent communication, including quick start instructions and task lifecycle management.
- Introduced `TokenManager.js` for managing authentication tokens in the client.
- Added data models for `Message.js`, `Profile.js`, and `Tweet.js` to represent Twitter's DM and user profile data, as well as tweet data from the GraphQL API.
- Created TypeScript declarations for the authentication module in `auth.d.ts`, defining interfaces and classes for cookie management, guest tokens, and session validation.

feat(linting): Add comprehensive linting tasks for MCP, API, Automation, and Utils

- Create detailed linting guides for fixing ESLint and Prettier violations in `src/mcp/`, `api/`, `src/automation/`, and remaining `src/` directories.
- Implement JSDoc requirements for tool handlers and route handlers.
- Establish EditorConfig and VS Code settings for consistent formatting across contributors.
- Set up a CI workflow in GitHub Actions to enforce linting and formatting checks on pushes and pull requests.

Add internationalization support and API for tweets and users

- Created i18n structure for translations in Japanese, Chinese, Spanish, Korean, and Brazilian Portuguese.
- Added full translations for README and quick start guides for each language.
- Implemented Tweets API with functions to get, send, delete, like, and retweet tweets.
- Implemented Users API with functions to get user profiles, followers, and following lists.
- Added input validation for usernames, tweet IDs, URLs, and other parameters.
fix(tests): update large buffer size in media upload flow test
feat(auth): implement TokenManager and storage integration for cookie management
feat: add GuestToken class for managing Twitter guest tokens and Message data model for direct messages

feat(auth): implement TokenManager for handling bearer and guest tokens

feat(auth): add TwoFactorAuth class for managing TOTP and SMS-based 2FA

feat(http): create RetryHandler for automatic retries with exponential backoff

feat(models): introduce Profile model for representing Twitter user profiles

feat(models): add Tweet model for representing tweets with metadata

test(a2a): add tests for agentCard functionality including card generation and diffing

test(a2a): implement tests for skillRegistry functions including conversion and searching

test(a2a): create tests for taskManager functionality including task creation and execution
feat: add input validation functions for usernames, tweet IDs, and text content

feat: add CookieJar class for managing Twitter session cookies
feat: create GraphQL query ID registry for Twitter API
feat: implement local tools with necessary imports for file and path handling
feat: add input validation functions for usernames, tweet IDs, and text content

feat: implement centralized error logging system with multiple transports

- Add ErrorLogger class for capturing and logging errors with context.
- Implement ConsoleTransport for logging to the console.
- Implement FileTransport for logging errors to JSON lines files.
- Implement MemoryTransport for in-memory error storage.
- Create singleton logger instance with environment-based configuration.
- Add tests for error logging functionality.

feat: build error recovery patterns for common failure scenarios

- Implement SessionRecovery class for handling session-related errors.
- Create withRecovery wrapper for retrying operations with recovery.
- Implement BrowserRecovery class for managing browser restarts.
- Add utility function for parsing cookie strings.
- Add tests for recovery patterns and their behaviors.

feat: add A2A push notification system with HMAC verification

- Implement PushNotificationServer for managing incoming notifications.
- Implement PushNotificationClient for sending notifications with retries.
- Create SubscriptionManager for managing callback subscriptions.
- Add methods for notifying task completion, failure, and progress.

feat: introduce SSE streaming for real-time task updates

- Implement StreamManager for managing multiple SSE streams.
- Create Stream class for handling individual task streams.
- Add bridgeTaskStream function to connect task lifecycle events to SSE.
- Implement attachStreamEndpoint function for exposing SSE endpoints.

test: add performance benchmarks for HTTP scraper

- Create benchmark tests comparing HTTP scraper speed against Puppeteer.
- Measure execution time for profile scraping, client creation, and data parsing.
- Log performance results for visibility and comparison.

test: add live integration tests for HTTP scraper

- Implement live tests for scraping Twitter profiles and followers.
- Validate guest token activation and header generation.
- Ensure tests are skipped by default and require valid Twitter cookies.

feat: add response parsers for Twitter GraphQL API

- Implemented robust parsing helpers for deeply nested GraphQL responses in `parsers.js`.
- Added functions to navigate responses, extract timeline entries, and parse tweet and user entries.
- Included media and poll parsing functionalities.

feat: create users API for user-related operations

- Introduced `users.js` to handle user profile retrieval, followers, and following actions.
- Implemented functions to get user by screen name and ID, and to follow/unfollow users.
- Added cursor-based pagination for fetching followers and following lists.

feat: implement token management for API requests

- Developed `TokenManager.js` to manage bearer, guest, and CSRF tokens for Twitter API.
- Included methods for activating guest tokens and building required headers for requests.

test: add comprehensive tests for TwitterHttpClient

- Created `client.test.js` to cover header construction, rate-limit detection, retry logic, cookie parsing, proxy URL handling, GraphQL URL construction, pagination, error class mapping, user-agent rotation, and unauthenticated requests.
- Utilized vitest for mocking fetch and ensuring no real network requests are made.
fix: Correct formatting and structure in Message.js to ensure proper parsing of Twitter DM entries

Add tests for HTTP Profile Scraper and Relationships Scraper

- Implement tests for parseUserData, scrapeProfile, and scrapeProfileById in profile.test.js
- Cover various user scenarios including complete, minimal, unavailable, and protected accounts
- Add tests for relationships scraping including followers, following, non-followers, likers, retweeters, and list members in relationships.test.js
- Utilize mock GraphQL responses to simulate Twitter API interactions
- Ensure comprehensive coverage of user data parsing and error handling

feat: Implement Twitter HTTP Scraper Modules

- Add GraphQL Endpoint Map & Constants for Twitter API
- Create Core HTTP Client for handling requests and rate limits
- Build Auth Token Manager for managing guest and authenticated sessions
- Implement Guest Token Flow for unauthenticated access to public API
- Develop Profile Scraping functionality via GraphQL
- Add Tweet Scraping capabilities for user tweets and threads
- Implement Follower/Following scraping with non-follower detection
- Introduce Show More Expander script for extracting full tweet text from the DOM
Refactor SVG graphics for xactions: update dimensions, styles, and animations; enhance demo SVG with improved layout and console output representation.
Update Procfile and railway.toml to use 'db push' for database deployment; add ASCII art SVG for branding
fix: use prisma db push instead of migrate deploy (no migrations folder)
Refactor sitemap.xml: Consolidate and update documentation URLs, adjust change frequencies and priorities, and remove outdated comments
Add Thought Leader Agent documentation with comprehensive overview, architecture, quick start guide, and module details

Add documentation for scraping, streaming, team management, video downloading, and video generation features

- Introduced `scraping.md` detailing the scraping infrastructure, including stealth browser, pagination engine, and proxy manager.
- Added `streams.md` for real-time Twitter monitoring, covering tweet, follower, and mention streams with CLI and API usage.
- Created `teams.md` for team management functionalities, including roles, permissions, and activity logging.
- Implemented `video-downloader.md` to explain video extraction and downloading from Twitter.
- Added `video-generation.md` for generating videos from Twitter data using Remotion.
- Included a demo SVG file for visual representation of terminal commands and outputs.

Add comprehensive SEO documentation for XActions

- Introduced a Press & Coverage Strategy document to guide independent coverage efforts.
- Created a Technical SEO Audit Checklist to ensure xactions.app is optimized for search engines.
- Developed a Structured Data Reference to define JSON-LD schemas for all web pages.
- Provided a Wikidata Setup Guide to facilitate the creation of a Wikidata item for XActions.
chore: add streaming/analytics exports, stage for Railway deploy

Add system status and use cases pages; document unfollow management scripts

- Created `status.html` for displaying the operational status of XActions services, including overall status, service details, and uptime history.
- Added `use-cases.html` to showcase various use cases for XActions, highlighting how different users leverage the tools.
- Introduced `script-details.md` to document algorithms and controls for unfollow management scripts, detailing functionalities and configurations for each script.
style: aesthetic README overhaul — banner, badges, terminal demo, polished sections
feat: add contact and support page with structured data and quick links
Refactor code structure for improved readability and maintainability
Add FAQ page, tutorial links, /faq route, fix vercel routing
Add project roadmap documentation for XActions

feat: v3.1.0 — plugins, workflows, cross-platform, streaming, analytics, graph, portability, extension

Biggest update since launch. 58,000+ lines across 115 files.

New systems:
- Plugin architecture (src/plugins/) — community npm packages
- Workflow engine (src/workflows/) — declarative JSON pipelines
- Cross-platform scrapers — Bluesky, Mastodon, Threads
- Real-time streaming (src/streaming/) — Socket.IO events
- Sentiment analysis (src/analytics/) — offline + LLM mode
- Social graph (src/graph/) — D3.js visualization
- Account portability (src/portability/) — export/migrate
- Browser extension (extension/) — Manifest V3

Also:
- 140+ MCP tools, 29 API routes, 15+ new browser scripts
- Dashboard: automations, monitor, workflows, analytics pages
- Docs site, TypeScript types, Docker support
- Version bump: 3.0.42 → 3.1.0

feat(agent): add Thought Leader Agent commands for start, stop, login, setup, and status
feat(dashboard): implement AI Agent dashboard with real-time metrics and activity feed
refactor(direct-messages): enhance DM skill description and update management scripts
feat(analytics): introduce competitive features for account and audience insights
milady
feat: add persona engine, trending topic monitor, and tweet A/B tester scripts; update dependencies in package.json

feat: add various scripts for managing X/Twitter functionalities

- Implemented leaveAllCommunities.js for bulk leaving communities with options for dry run and whitelisting.
- Created listManager.js for managing Twitter lists, including creating lists, adding members, and exporting members.
- Developed notificationManager.js to scrape and filter notifications based on user-defined criteria.
- Introduced premiumManager.js to detect and report on Premium tier features and account status.
- Added sendDirectMessage.js for sending personalized DMs to multiple users with a configurable message template.
- Built settingsManager.js for exporting account settings and toggling account protection.
- Created spacesManager.js for scraping live and scheduled Spaces on X/Twitter.
- Added algorithm internals and architecture documentation for algorithm cultivation.
- Included references for browser scripts and persona engine to aid in algorithm cultivation.

Add various scripts for Twitter account monitoring and analysis

- Introduced monitorAccount.js for tracking follows/unfollows on public accounts.
- Added sentimentAnalyzer.js to analyze tweet sentiment using a basic lexicon.
- Created tweetPerformance.js for ranking tweets by engagement and categorizing by type.
- Implemented tweetScheduleOptimizer.js for generating an optimal weekly posting schedule.
- Developed viralTweetDetector.js for detecting tweets with unusually high engagement.
- Added profileManager.js for managing user profiles (file created, functionality to be implemented).

feat: add scripts for unliking posts and analyzing tweet-price correlation

- Implemented `unlikeAllPosts.js` for bulk unliking of posts on X/Twitter with configurable options.
- Created `priceCorrelation.js` to analyze the relationship between tweet activity and crypto price movements, fetching data from CoinGecko and GeckoTerminal.
- Introduced `contentRepurposer.js` to transform tweets into various content formats, enhancing content ROI through repurposing strategies.

feat: add Smithery/Glama MCP badges, TypeScript types, release workflow, API reference, issue templates

- Add Smithery and Glama MCP directory badges to README
- Add TypeScript type declarations (types/index.d.ts)
- Add types field to package.json
- Add GitHub Release automation workflow (.github/workflows/release.yml)
- Add API reference documentation (docs/api-reference.md)
- Add issue template config with docs/community links
- Include new browser scripts and analytics modules
milady

feat: Add persona management commands for algorithm building

- Implemented commands for creating, listing, running, editing, deleting, and checking status of personas.
- Introduced interactive setup for persona creation with options for presets, strategies, and activity patterns.
- Enhanced persona details with custom topics, search terms, and target accounts.
- Added engagement strategies and activity patterns for persona configuration.

feat: Introduce Engagement Leaderboard script

- Created a script to analyze tweet engagement, identifying top repliers, likers, and retweeters.
- Outputs a leaderboard and VIP list for relationship building, along with engagement tiers and performance analysis.

feat: Add Crawlee adapter for advanced scraping

- Developed a Crawlee adapter for smart crawling with features like request queuing, proxy rotation, and session management.
- Supports both Puppeteer and Playwright for browser automation.

chore: Add placeholder files for got-jsdom adapter and trending topic monitor
Refactor code structure for improved readability and maintainability

feat: add scripts for unfollowing users and detecting unfollowers on X/Twitter

- Implemented detectUnfollowers.js to identify users who have unfollowed you.
- Created unfollowEveryone.js to unfollow all users with configurable options.
- Developed unfollowWDFBLog.js for unfollowing non-followers with detailed logging.
- Added unfollowback.js to unfollow users who do not follow back.
- Introduced Excel plugin for exporting scraped data to styled Excel files.
- Added Google Sheets plugin for direct export of scraped data to Google Sheets.
milady

- Implement graph building logic to crawl user connections

- Introduce recommendations based on graph analysis
- Add visualizer for exporting graph data to D3.js, GEXF, and HTML formats
- Establish storage functions for saving and loading graphs
- Configure Cloudflare Pages for static frontend deployment

feat(graph): implement social graph builder with crawling, serialization, and visualization

- Implemented voice analysis functionality in `voiceAnalyzer.js` to create a VoiceProfile from user tweets.
- Developed tweet generation capabilities in `tweetGenerator.js`, including functions for generating tweets, threads, rewrites, weekly content calendars, and replies.
- Created a public API in `index.js` to expose the voice analysis and tweet generation functionalities.
- Added comprehensive documentation and comments throughout the codebase for clarity and maintainability.

commit.message:
Add tutorials for brand monitoring, CLI mastery, browser automation, and media downloading with XActions

- Created a comprehensive tutorial on brand monitoring, business tools, and customer service using XActions.
- Developed a CLI mastery guide detailing installation, commands, and workflows for XActions.
- Introduced a browser automation framework tutorial covering core modules, actions, and automation scripts.
- Added a tutorial for downloading videos, unrolling threads, and exporting media from X using XActions.

feat: Implement XActions Workflow Engine with CRUD operations and execution logging

- Added core workflow engine (engine.js) to manage workflow execution, context, and logging.
- Introduced high-level workflow API (index.js) for creating, updating, and running workflows.
- Developed a persistent store (store.js) supporting both JSON file and Prisma/PostgreSQL backends for workflow definitions and execution logs.
- Created tutorials for scraping, analysis, content posting, and scheduling with XActions.

feat: add XActions Stream Manager and related utilities for real-time event streaming

- Implemented stream management with Bull for scheduling and Redis for state persistence.
- Created tweet, follower, and mention polling functionalities.
- Added core utilities for XActions including rate-limiting, state persistence, and DOM helpers.
- Introduced a tutorial for setting up the XActions MCP server with Claude.
Add comprehensive SEO prompts for image optimization, link building, developer platform SEO, social media distribution, AI search optimization, performance monitoring, and analytics setup

Add scripts for Twitter automation and data management

- Introduced backupAccount.js for exporting Twitter account data as JSON.
- Added autoEngage.js for automated engagement actions (likes, bookmarks).
- Created dmExporter.js to export direct message conversations.
- Implemented editProfile.js for updating Twitter profile fields.
- Developed manageBookmarks.js for exporting and managing bookmarks.
- Added manageLists.js for list management (file created).
- Created manageNotifications.js to scrape and manage notifications.
- Introduced postComposer.js for composing and posting tweets, threads, and polls.
- Added publishArticle.js for article publishing (file created).
- Implemented scrapeAnalytics.js for scraping Twitter analytics data.
- Created scrapeExplore.js for scraping Explore page trends and content.
- Added scrapeSpaces.js for scraping Spaces content (file created).
- Added example documentation for backup account functionality.
- Created structured data prompts for SEO optimization.
Add new SEO prompts and scripts for follower analysis and engagement optimization

Add various scripts for enhanced X platform interactions

- Implement postThread.js for composing and posting a thread of tweets.
- Create qrCodeSharing.js to generate and share QR codes for X profiles.
- Develop removeFollowers.js to soft-block specific followers or all visible ones.
- Introduce reportSpam.js for reporting multiple spam accounts efficiently.
- Add schedulePosts.js for queuing multiple posts to be published at specified times.
- Implement scrapeSpaces.js to collect metadata about X Spaces.
- Create sendDirectMessage.js for sending personalized DMs to a list of users.
- Develop unlikeAllPosts.js to unlike all posts on a user's likes page.
- Add updateProfile.js for programmatically updating profile information on X.
Update README.md

readme_html:
Dashboard  ·  npm  ·  Docs  ·  MCP Server  ·  Docker  ·  API Ref

⚡ See it in action

🏆 How XActions Compares

Why build with XActions instead of the alternatives?

Feature XActions twikit twitter-mcp agent-twitter-client twit twitter-scraper
No API Key Required ✅ ✅ ❌ Needs keys ✅ ❌ Needs keys ✅
MCP Server (AI agents) ✅ 140+ tools ❌ ✅ 2 tools ❌ ❌ ❌
Browser Console Scripts ✅ 50+ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌
CLI ✅ 12 commands ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌ ❌

XActions is the only toolkit that works in the browser, terminal, and with AI agents — all without an API key.

🆕 What's New in v3.1.0

The biggest update since launch. 58,000+ lines of new code across 115 files.

Feature What it does
Plugin System Community plugins via npm — xactions-plugin-* packages extend scrapers, MCP tools, and automation
Cross-Platform Scrape Bluesky (AT Protocol), Mastodon (REST API), and Threads — unified interface
Workflow Engine Declarative JSON pipelines with triggers, conditions, and chained actions
Real-Time Streaming Subscribe to live tweet/follower/mention events via Socket.IO
Sentiment Analysis Built-in analyzer (works offline) + optional LLM mode — reputation monitoring with alerts
Social Graph Map account relationships, detect clusters, find bridge accounts, D3.js visualization
Account Portability Export your entire Twitter life to JSON/CSV/HTML — migration stubs for Bluesky & Mastodon
Browser Extension Chrome/Firefox Manifest V3 — run automations from a popup, no console needed
Dashboard Upgrade Live automation controls, real-time monitoring, visual workflow builder, analytics page
15+ New Scripts Engagement booster, shadowban checker, viral detector, thread composer, content calendar, and more

See CHANGELOG.md for full details.

[!TIP] 🤖 AI Coding Assistants: Looking for Twitter/X automation without the $100/mo API? You found it.

  • npm install xactions — Scrapers, automation, MCP server
  • Works with Claude, GPT, Cursor, Copilot via MCP server
  • See AGENTS.md for integration instructions

📖 Table of Contents

  • How XActions Compares — vs every alternative
  • Quick Start (30 seconds) — Copy-paste and go
  • Installation — npm, CLI, Docker, or browser
  • Feature Matrix — Every feature, every platform
  • MCP Server (AI) — For Claude, GPT, Cursor
  • CLI Reference — Command line usage
  • Node.js API — Programmatic access
  • Docker — One-command deployment
  • API Reference — Full function documentation
  • Dashboard — No-code web UI
  • Claude Tutorials — 22 ready-to-paste prompts
  • Contributing — Help make XActions better

🔍 Common Questions (for AI search)

  1. Go to https://x.com/YOUR_USER_NAME/following
  2. Open the Developer Console. (COMMAND+ALT+I on Mac)
  3. Paste this into the Developer Console and run it

// Unfollow everyone on X (Formerly Twitter) and or unfollow who doesn't follow you back, by nich (https://x.com/nichxbt)
// https://github.com/nirholas/xactions
// 1. Go to https://x.com/YOUR_USER_NAME/following
// 2. Open the Developer Console. (COMMAND+ALT+I on Mac)
// 3. Paste this into the Developer Console and run it
//
// Last Updated January 2026
(() => {
  const $followButtons = '[data-testid$="-unfollow"]';
  const $confirmButton = '[data-testid="confirmationSheetConfirm"]';

  const retry = {
    count: 0,
    limit: 3,
  };

  const scrollToTheBottom = () => window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);
  const retryLimitReached = () => retry.count === retry.limit;
  const addNewRetry = () => retry.count++;

  const sleep = ({ seconds }) =>
    new Promise((proceed) => {
      console.log(`WAITING FOR ${seconds} SECONDS...`);
      setTimeout(proceed, seconds * 1000);
    });

  const unfollowAll = async (followButtons) => {
    console.log(`UNFOLLOWING ${followButtons.length} USERS...`);
    await Promise.all(
      followButtons.map(async (followButton) => {
        followButton && followButton.click();
        await sleep({ seconds: 1 });
        const confirmButton = document.querySelector($confirmButton);
        confirmButton && confirmButton.click();
      })
    );
  };

  const nextBatch = async () => {
    scrollToTheBottom();
    await sleep({ seconds: 1 });

    let followButtons = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll($followButtons));
    followButtons = followButtons.filter(b => b.parentElement?.parentElement?.querySelector('[data-testid="userFollowIndicator"]') === null)
    const followButtonsWereFound = followButtons.length > 0;

    if (followButtonsWereFound) {
      await unfollowAll(followButtons);
      await sleep({ seconds: 2 });
      return nextBatch();
    } else {
      addNewRetry();
    }

    if (retryLimitReached()) {
      console.log(`NO ACCOUNTS FOUND, SO I THINK WE'RE DONE`);
      console.log(`RELOAD PAGE AND RE-RUN SCRIPT IF ANY WERE MISSED`);
    } else {
      await sleep({ seconds: 2 });
      return nextBatch();
    }
  };

  nextBatch();
})();

Or use the dashboard for a visual interface.

  1. Go to https://x.com/YOUR_USER_NAME/following

    new Promise((proceed) => {

    console.log(`UNFOLLOWING ${followButtons.length} USERS...`);

        followButton && followButton.click();
        await sleep({ seconds: 1 });
        const confirmButton = document.querySelector($confirmButton);
        confirmButton && confirmButton.click();
      })
    );
  };

  const nextBatch = async () => {
    scrollToTheBottom();
    await sleep({ seconds: 1 });

    const followButtons = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll($followButtons));
    const followButtonsWereFound = followButtons.length > 0;

    if (followButtonsWereFound) {
      await unfollowAll(followButtons);
      await sleep({ seconds: 2 });
      return nextBatch();
    } else {

    }

      console.log(`NO ACCOUNTS FOUND, SO I THINK WE'RE DONE`);
      console.log(`RELOAD PAGE AND RE-RUN SCRIPT IF ANY WERE MISSED`);
    } else {

      return nextBatch();
    }
  };

})();

This script:

  • Is completely free.
  • Doesn't try and get you to sign in or take your personal data.
  • Automates your web browser to make it click unfollow buttons, scroll down to reveal more, then do it again.
  • No tricks, all of the code is here so you can see exactly what it does.

Use src/detectUnfollowers.js - it saves a snapshot of your followers and compares on next run.

Use src/scrapers/videoDownloader.js - extracts MP4 URLs from any tweet.

XActions uses browser automation instead of the API. No API keys needed, no rate limits, no $100/mo fee.

XActions is open-source and completely free for humans. AI agents pay micropayments per request.

🚀 Quick Start Examples

Example 1: Unfollow Non-Followers (30 seconds)

Browser Console — No install required!

// Go to: x.com/YOUR_USERNAME/following
// Press F12 → Console → Paste this:

(() => {
  const sleep = (s) => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, s * 1000));
  const run = async () => {
    const buttons = [...document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid$="-unfollow"]')]
      .filter(b => !b.closest('[data-testid="UserCell"]')
        ?.querySelector('[data-testid="userFollowIndicator"]'));
    
    for (const btn of buttons) {
      btn.click();
      await sleep(1);
      document.querySelector('[data-testid="confirmationSheetConfirm"]')?.click();
      await sleep(2);
    }
    window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);
    await sleep(2);
    if (document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid$="-unfollow"]').length) run();
    else console.log('✅ Done! Reload page to continue.');
  };
  run();
})();

CLI:

xactions login
xactions non-followers YOUR_USERNAME --output non-followers.json

Node.js:

import { createBrowser, createPage, scrapeFollowing } from 'xactions';

const browser = await createBrowser();
const page = await createPage(browser);
const following = await scrapeFollowing(page, 'your_username', { limit: 500 });
const nonFollowers = following.filter(u => !u.followsBack);
console.log(`Found ${nonFollowers.length} non-followers`);
await browser.close();

💡 Don't want to code? Use xactions.app — just login and click!

Example 4: Detect Who Unfollowed You

Browser Console:

// Go to: x.com/YOUR_USERNAME/followers

(() => {
  const KEY = 'xactions_followers';
  const sleep = (ms) => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms));
  
  const scrape = async () => {
    const users = new Set();
    let retries = 0;
    while (retries < 5) {
      document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="UserCell"] a')
        .forEach(a => users.add(a.href.split('/')[3]?.toLowerCase()));
      window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);
      await sleep(1500);
      retries++;
    }
    return [...users].filter(Boolean);
  };

  scrape().then(current => {
    const saved = localStorage.getItem(KEY);
    if (saved) {
      const old = JSON.parse(saved);
      const gone = old.filter(u => !current.includes(u));
      console.log('🚨 Unfollowed you:', gone);
    }
    localStorage.setItem(KEY, JSON.stringify(current));
    console.log(`💾 Saved ${current.length} followers`);
  });
})();

CLI:

# First run saves snapshot
xactions followers YOUR_USERNAME --output snapshot1.json

# Later, compare
xactions followers YOUR_USERNAME --output snapshot2.json
# Use diff tools to compare

Example 5: Auto-Like Posts by Keyword

Browser Console:

// Go to: x.com/search?q=YOUR_KEYWORD&f=live

(async () => {
  const sleep = (s) => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, s * 1000));
  const liked = new Set();
  
  while (liked.size < 20) { // Like 20 posts
    const buttons = [...document.querySelectorAll('[data-testid="like"]')]
      .filter(b => !liked.has(b));
    
    for (const btn of buttons.slice(0, 3)) {
      btn.click();
      liked.add(btn);
      console.log(`❤️ Liked ${liked.size} posts`);
      await sleep(3 + Math.random() * 2); // Random delay
    }
    window.scrollTo(0, document.body.scrollHeight);
    await sleep(2);
  }
  console.log('✅ Done!');
})();

⚠️ Go slow! Twitter may rate-limit you. The website version handles this automatically.

Example 6: Leave All Communities

Browser Console:

// Go to: x.com/YOUR_USERNAME/communities

(() => {
  const $communityLinks = 'a[href^="/i/communities/"]';
  const $joinedButton = 'button[aria-label^="Joined"]';
  const $confirmButton = '[data-testid="confirmationSheetConfirm"]';
  const $communitiesNav = 'a[aria-label="Communities"]';

  const getLeftCommunities = () => {
    try { return JSON.parse(sessionStorage.getItem('xactions_left_ids') || '[]'); }
    catch { return []; }
  };
  const markAsLeft = (id) => {
    const left = getLeftCommunities();
    if (!left.includes(id)) {
      left.push(id);
      sessionStorage.setItem('xactions_left_ids', JSON.stringify(left));
    }
  };

  const sleep = (ms) => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms));
  const getCommunityId = () => {
    const leftAlready = getLeftCommunities();
    for (const link of document.querySelectorAll($communityLinks)) {
      const match = link.href.match(/\/i\/communities\/(\d+)/);
      if (match && !leftAlready.includes(match[1])) return { id: match[1], element: link };
    }
    return null;
  };

  const run = async () => {
    console.log(`🚀 Left so far: ${getLeftCommunities().length}`);
    await sleep(1500);
    const joinedBtn = document.querySelector($joinedButton);
    if (joinedBtn) {
      const urlMatch = window.location.href.match(/\/i\/communities\/(\d+)/);
      const currentId = urlMatch ? urlMatch[1] : null;
      joinedBtn.click();
      await sleep(1000);
      const confirmBtn = document.querySelector($confirmButton);
      if (confirmBtn) { confirmBtn.click(); if (currentId) markAsLeft(currentId); await sleep(1500); }
      const communitiesLink = document.querySelector($communitiesNav);
      if (communitiesLink) { communitiesLink.click(); await sleep(2500); return run(); }
    }
    const community = getCommunityId();
    if (community) { community.element.click(); await sleep(2500); return run(); }
    else { console.log(`🎉 DONE! Left ${getLeftCommunities().length} communities`); sessionStorage.removeItem('xactions_left_ids'); }
  };
  run();
})();

🤖 MCP Server (AI Agents)

XActions includes the most comprehensive free MCP server for X/Twitter. Works with Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and any MCP client.

Quick Setup (30 seconds)

Add to your Claude Desktop config (claude_desktop_config.json):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "xactions": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "xactions-mcp"],
      "env": {
        "XACTIONS_SESSION_COOKIE": "your_auth_token_here"
      }
    }
  }
}

Get your auth_token: x.com → DevTools (F12) → Application → Cookies → copy auth_token value

Or auto-generate the config:

npx xactions mcp-config --client claude
npx xactions mcp-config --client cursor
npx xactions mcp-config --client windsurf

📚 Node.js API

Quick Start

import { 
  createBrowser, 
  createPage, 
  loginWithCookie,
  scrapeProfile,
  scrapeFollowers,
  scrapeFollowing,
  scrapeTweets,
  searchTweets,
  exportToJSON,
  exportToCSV 
} from 'xactions';

// Initialize
const browser = await createBrowser({ headless: true });
const page = await createPage(browser);

// Optional: Login for private data
await loginWithCookie(page, 'your_auth_token_cookie');

// Scrape profile
const profile = await scrapeProfile(page, 'elonmusk');

// Scrape followers with progress
const followers = await scrapeFollowers(page, 'elonmusk', {
  limit: 1000,
  onProgress: ({ scraped, limit }) => console.log(`${scraped}/${limit}`)
});

// Export data
await exportToJSON(followers, 'followers.json');
await exportToCSV(followers, 'followers.csv');

await browser.close();

All Scraper Functions

// Profile
scrapeProfile(page, username)

// Followers & Following
scrapeFollowers(page, username, { limit, onProgress })
scrapeFollowing(page, username, { limit, onProgress })

// Tweets
scrapeTweets(page, username, { limit, includeReplies, onProgress })
searchTweets(page, query, { limit, filter: 'latest'|'top' })
scrapeThread(page, tweetUrl)
scrapeHashtag(page, hashtag, { limit, filter })

// Media
scrapeMedia(page, username, { limit })
scrapeLikes(page, tweetUrl, { limit })

// Lists
scrapeListMembers(page, listUrl, { limit })

// Export
exportToJSON(data, filename)
exportToCSV(data, filename)

Don't: Mass-follow thousands/day · Run 24/7 · Spam comments

npm run cli -- profile elonmusk   # Run CLI locally
npm run mcp                       # Run MCP server

🚀 One-Click Script Runner

NEW! Run scripts without any coding knowledge:

  1. Visit xactions.app/run.html
  2. Drag any blue button to your bookmarks bar
  3. Go to x.com and click the bookmarklet

No console, no code, no setup!

🌐 Live HTTP Deployment

XActions is deployed and accessible over HTTP via MCP Streamable HTTP transport — no local installation required.

Endpoint:

https://modelcontextprotocol.name/mcp/xactions

Connect from any MCP Client

Add to your MCP client configuration (Claude Desktop, Cursor, SperaxOS, etc.):

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "xactions": {
      "type": "http",
      "url": "https://modelcontextprotocol.name/mcp/xactions"
    }
  }
}

Available Tools (3)

Tool Description
search_twitter_users Search X/Twitter profiles
get_twitter_trends Trending topics
analyze_social_sentiment Social sentiment analysis

Example Requests

Search X/Twitter profiles:

curl -X POST https://modelcontextprotocol.name/mcp/xactions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"search_twitter_users","arguments":{"query":"crypto"}}}'

Trending topics:

curl -X POST https://modelcontextprotocol.name/mcp/xactions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"get_twitter_trends","arguments":{"query":"bitcoin"}}}'

Social sentiment analysis:

curl -X POST https://modelcontextprotocol.name/mcp/xactions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"analyze_social_sentiment","arguments":{"topic":"ethereum"}}}'

List All Tools

curl -X POST https://modelcontextprotocol.name/mcp/xactions \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"tools/list"}'

Also Available On

  • All 27 MCP servers — See the full catalog at modelcontextprotocol.name
